why: The admission request primary key is 32 LOWERCASE hex digits, no dashes (REQUEST_ID_RE cortex validate.ts). Baseline accept.
requested_scope is built as federated.${principal}.> (principals.ts). Baseline round-trip of the reserved federated prefix + principal segment + '>' tail.

The scope delimiter is '.', not '-', so a hyphen-bearing principal round-trips unambiguously here — unlike the did:mf:{principal}-{stack} join whose '-' separator collides with in-principal hyphens (RFC-0001). Proves the scope decode is NOT vulnerable to the hyphen-separator defect.
ADMITTED is the roster-membership source of truth (ADR-0018 Q3). One of the five reserved enum tokens (types.ts).
DEPARTED (member left) and REVOKED (hub kicked) are DISTINCT terminal states the roster must keep separable (admission-read.ts). Collapsing them loses the left-vs-kicked audit distinction.

WIDENED replacement for the superseded decision-claim/canonical-bytes-admit (D6, App-C 2026-07-14). Both parties MUST agree byte-for-byte (canonical-JSON: sort-keys, no whitespace, undefined skipped) — canonicalization now RFC-0004-owned (§7.2, RFC-0004 §3/§4.4 + CONTEXT_TAG). The signed bytes now BIND the admitted identity: peer_pubkey AND network_id are inside the signed message, so the admin signature cryptographically commits to WHICH key on WHICH network is admitted, closing OD-2 (§7.1/§7.3). Both bound fields are encoding-stable (peer_pubkey = raw base64 Ed25519, network_id = plain string, never a did:mf terminal) so neither flips at the RFC-0001 §9 DID flag-day — the widening runs its OWN dual-accept window.

§7.3 accept limb: an admit whose bound peer_pubkey AND network_id both equal the target PENDING row's fields is authorized to proceed (subject to the §7.4 authority check). Baseline positive for enforceDecisionIdentityBinding.

§7.3 DUAL-ACCEPT window (D7), decoupled from the RFC-0001 §9 flag-day. A pre-widening (narrow) claim that OMITS peer_pubkey + network_id MUST still be accepted (enforce-WHEN-present), so pre-upgrade signers keep working. The verifier flips to require-present — rejecting a narrow claim — only after every signer upgrades, an explicit dated step on the OD-2 issue gated on a monitored narrow-claim counter reading zero. Nearly free via the #1414 verify-over-canonicalJSON(signed.claim)-as-received pattern (an added signed field is transparently verified by an unchanged verifier).

WIDENED replacement for the superseded sealed-secret-claim/canonical-bytes (D8, App-C 2026-07-14). The hub-admin's SealedSecretWriteClaim (second party of the two-party gate, ADR-0018 Q5; hub = did:mf:hub.testnet) now BINDS peer_pubkey — the target stack key the ciphertext is sealed to — into the signed bytes, symmetric with the registry-admin decision claim (§7.3): the gate cannot have one side bind identity and the other not. Pins the canonical-JSON bytes (RFC-0004-owned, §7.2) INCLUDING peer_pubkey. Defense-in-depth + audit/intent signal, NOT the fix for identity substitution — crypto_box_seal carries the PRIMARY targeting guarantee.

§8.3 accept limb: a seal-delivery write whose bound peer_pubkey equals the addressed sealed_secrets[] entry's target_stack_pubkey is authorized. Baseline positive for enforceSealWriteBinding under the per-stack sealed_secrets[] delivery of §8.1 (D2).

§8.1 R7 accept limb (fetch-seam). A v2 credential whose decoded leaf_user IS among the caller's expected member identities installs. Baseline positive binding the install-time comparison the reference impl enforces (cortex fetch-sealed-secret.ts:132 expected.includes(env.leaf_user)).

§4.2 depart (D5, ratification-gate). A member-PoP depart of an ADMITTED row transitions ADMITTED -> DEPARTED and MUST clear BOTH sealed_secret AND hub_authorized_at — a departed member is no longer hub-authorized, so the liveness stamp MUST NOT survive. Symmetric with revoke and aligned to §5/§8.2. Binds the deployed transition (cortex store.ts:452-458 sets status DEPARTED + both fields null). The depart is authorized by proof over the row's peer_pubkey; a member MUST NOT depart another's row.

§4.2 revoke (companion to D5). A hub-admin revoke of an ADMITTED row transitions ADMITTED -> REVOKED and MUST clear BOTH fields, symmetric with depart (cortex store.ts:434-440). REVOKED (kicked) stays DISTINCT from DEPARTED (left). NOTE per §12 the registry mark is necessary-not-sufficient: a complete revocation also cuts transport (leaf credential invalidation) and rotates the per-network payload key K (ADR-0019) — those are out-of-band of this row transition.

§4.2 register-idempotency corner of D3. Re-registering the SAME (principal_id, peer_pubkey, network_id) triple returns the EXISTING row unchanged — it MUST NOT reset a terminal DEPARTED row back to PENDING. Re-admission is key rotation: the peer generates a FRESH peer_pubkey, which mints a NEW triple and therefore a new PENDING row; the old terminal row stays immutable and its audit trail is preserved. This closes the 'just re-register' dead-end (network.ts:1019/1025 CLI copy).

§4.2 ADMITTED sub-lifecycle DERIVED (D4). The 'unsealed' stage is READ OFF field presence (sealed_secret == null), NOT an enum token. status stays ADMITTED — the enum MUST NOT grow a sixth token for the stages within ADMITTED.

§4.2 ADMITTED sub-lifecycle DERIVED (D4). The 'sealed' stage is READ OFF field presence (sealed_secret != null AND hub_authorized_at == null), NOT a distinct enum value. seal/authorize are FIELD STAMPS that do not change status; the projection keeps status monotonic. A conforming impl MUST compute the sub-lifecycle from (sealed_secret, hub_authorized_at) presence and MUST NOT introduce a sixth AdmissionStatus token.

§4.2 ADMITTED sub-lifecycle DERIVED (D4). The terminal 'hub-authorized' stage is READ OFF both fields present (sealed_secret != null AND hub_authorized_at != null). status still ADMITTED. Completes the monotonic unsealed -> sealed -> hub-authorized projection — three derived stages over ONE status token, never five-plus-one enum values.

§4.2 covered-by-principal DERIVED READOUT (D1), scoped to the register READ path where --principal-seed is in hand. A principal-level projection over the principal's admission rows surfaces 'covered by the principal's admission' to fix the phantom-PENDING readout (#1748 register half). It is a DISPLAY projection only: derived at read time, never persisted as a status token, and join_gate_input MUST be false — it MUST NOT be consulted to admit transport.

§4.2 / §9 boundary of D1. On the member-PoP /mine path (peer_pubkey-only authority, no principal seed) the covered-by-principal readout is NOT available: there is no §9-safe read by which a second stack holding ONLY its own key learns its principal is covered. /mine MUST keep its peer_pubkey-only authority and MUST NOT be widened to key on principal_id. This vector guards against that widening.
v1 is the sovereign-model transport PSK envelope (ADR-0018). Decoders MUST accept it. The sealed plaintext is UTF-8 JSON, version-pinned by v.

The ADR-0019 payload key K rides the SAME sealed slot as an OPTIONAL rider. Decoders MUST tolerate its presence AND absence (sealed-leaf-secret.ts readPayloadKeyFields) — one extra sealed blob, no schema change.

v2 is the operator-mode scoped-user .creds envelope that SUPERSEDED the v1 PSK (ADR-0023, epic #1595). The payload variant is DISCRIMINATED by v (not leaf_psk-XOR-creds) so a version-blind decoder cannot be tricked into a silent payload downgrade (design R9/R12).
